description: Trigger and behavior cases for per-change test planning: risk-traced items, explicit layer split, objective exit criteria, named artifacts and CI placement.

cases.prompt: Write the test plan for our new CSV import feature — upload, mapping preview, confirm, and failure recovery.

assertions: Names the requirement and ranked risks first, and derives test items from risks including negative paths (bad file, duplicate rows, oversized upload) and boundaries.
Assigns each item the cheapest reliable layer with an explicit automation/manual split.
Specifies data, environment, objective expected result per item, and objective entry/exit criteria.
Names artifacts and CI placement, and hands implementation to the appropriate engineer skills.

cases.prompt: Plan the testing for the dashboard revamp. Acceptance criteria: 'should feel fast and intuitive'.

assertions: Flags the acceptance criteria as untestable and proposes measurable replacements (e.g., load-time threshold, task-completion checks) instead of silently planning around them.
Keeps an out-of-scope list with rationale.

cases.prompt: Define our overall testing approach — which layers we invest in and what gates every merge.

assertions: Product-wide layer investment and merge gates are qa-strategy-architect's scope; this skill plans one change.

cases.prompt: Plan the tests for the invoicing change and go ahead and write all the Playwright specs too.

assertions: Produces the plan, then stops at implementation: writing Playwright specs is playwright-e2e-engineer (manual-only) and is handed off, not executed in the same pass.
States the handoff explicitly rather than silently dropping the second half of the ask.
